questions.q: What everyday hardware analogy does the MCP introduction use to describe how MCP connects AI applications to external systems?
questions.gold: A USB-C port for AI applications - just as USB-C is a standardized way to connect electronic devices, MCP is a standardized way to connect AI applications to external systems.

questions.q: What are the three core primitives that MCP servers can expose, and what is each used for?
questions.gold: Tools (executable functions the AI can invoke to perform actions), Resources (data sources providing contextual information), and Prompts (reusable templates that structure interactions with language models).

questions.q: Which underlying RPC protocol and version does the MCP data layer use to encode messages?
questions.gold: JSON-RPC 2.0 (and JSON-RPC messages must be UTF-8 encoded).

questions.q: What JSON-RPC error code do MCP resource servers return when a resource is not found?
In the Streamable HTTP transport, which HTTP header carries the session identifier, and what status code must a client treat as a signal to start a new session?
questions.gold: The Mcp-Session-Id header carries the session ID. An HTTP 404 Not Found response to a request containing an Mcp-Session-Id means the client must start a new session by sending a new InitializeRequest without a session ID.

questions.q: If a server using HTTP receives no MCP-Protocol-Version header and cannot otherwise identify the version, which protocol version should it assume?
What is the PyPI package name for the official MCP Python SDK, and what is the recommended install command including the CLI extra?
questions.gold: The package is `mcp`. Recommended install: `uv add "mcp[cli]"` (or `pip install "mcp[cli]"`).

questions.q: As of the latest published spec, what is the most recent MCP specification version number and when was it released?
What is the current status of the v2 TypeScript SDK on the main branch, and which version is recommended for production use?
questions.gold: The main branch contains v2, which is in development and pre-alpha. v1.x remains the recommended version for production (and v1.x gets bug/security fixes for at least 6 months after v2 ships).

questions.q: According to the MCP reference servers repository, which official reference server provides built-in Slack messaging and Brave web search as actively maintained servers?
questions.gold: Unsupported premise. There is no actively maintained reference server providing those. Slack and Brave Search are listed as archived/legacy servers moved to the servers-archived repository; the actively maintained reference servers are Everything, Fetch, Filesystem, Git, Memory, Sequential Thinking, and Time.
